identificationModule.briefTitle: Enhancing Inpatient Psychotherapeutic Treatment With Online Self-help : Acceptance and Efficacy
identificationModule.officialTitle: Enhancing Inpatient Psychotherapeutic Treatment With Online Self Help in a Randomised Controlled Trial: Acceptance and Efficacy

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to determine the acceptance and efficacy of the online self-help program deprexis® for depressed patients in reducing the clinical symptoms of their depression. The patients of the experimental group use deprexis® for 90 days, the patients of the placebo group receive weekly online information about depression for 90 days as well. Both groups receive their treatment in addition to their regular inpatient psychosomatic treatment and as an aftercare intervention.The investigators hypothesize that the online self-help group achieves a greater reduction of depression compared to the control group.

armGroups.label: deprexis®
armGroups.description: Patients in this arm use deprexis® as an additional treatment during their inpatient stay and as an aftercare intervention.

armGroups.label: Information
armGroups.description: Patients in this arm receive online information about depression as a placebo comparator in addition to their treatment during their inpatient stay and as an aftercare intervention.

interventions.name: deprexis®
interventions.description: Online self-help program in addition to inpatient psychosomatic treatment for 90 days.

interventions.name: Information
interventions.description: The patients receive online information about depression once a week for 90 days.

primaryOutcomes.measure: Reduction of depression measured with the "Beck's Depression Inventory" (BDI-II)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Reduction of depression measured with the depression scale of the "Patient Health Questionnaire" (PHQ-9)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Reduction of depression measured with the "Beck's Depression Inventory" (BDI-II)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Reduction of anxiety measured with the "Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale" (GAD-7)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Reduction of dysfunctional depression related cognitions measured with the "Dysfunctional Attitude Scale" (DAS)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Improved self-esteem measured with the "Rosenberg Self-Esteem Scale" (RSE)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Improved quality of life measured with the "European Health Interview Survey Quality of Life-8" (EUROHIS-QOL 8)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Improved working ability measured with the short form of the "Work Ability Index" (WAI)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), six months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Acceptance and utilization of deprexis® measured with a self devised questionnaire
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: End of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), three months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Acceptance and utilization of the information used in the placebo condition measured with a self devised questionnaire
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: End of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), three months after randomization

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Degree of childhood traumas measured with the "Childhood Trauma Questionnaire" (CTQ)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Assumption that childhood traumas, assessed at the beginning of treatment, have a moderating effect on the reduction in the primary outcome measure.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Degree of structural deficits measured with the short form of the OPD-Structure Questionnaire (OPD-SFK)
secondaryOutcomes.description: Assumption that structural deficits, as assessed with the OPD-SFK questionnaire at the beginning of treatment have a moderating effect on the reduction in the primary outcome measure.

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Therapeutic alliance between participant and inpatient psychotherapist measured with the "Helping Alliance Questionnaire" (HAQ).
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Randomization, end of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Course of mood, depressiveness and utilization of units
Utilization of other treatments after the end of inpatient treatment

secondaryOutcomes.description: BDI score below the cutoff of 13 and a reliable change regarding Reliable Change Index (RCI)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: End of inpatient treatment (average 6 weeks), three months after randomization, six months after randomization

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Inpatient treatment
* Private internet access
* Informed consent
* Age between 18 and 65 years
* Knowledge of the German language
* Score in the BDI-II \> 13 and clinical diagnosis of a depression (ICD-10: F32.x, F33.x, F34.1, F43.2) verified by the inpatient therapist

Exclusion Criteria:

* Psychosis
* Current alcohol or drug dependency
* Borderline, antisocial, schizoid or schizotypal personality disorder
* Anorexia nervosa
* Life time diagnosis of a schizophrenia, schizoaffective , bipolar oder organic psychic disorder
